title: Dot Product and Cross Product
summary: Scalar (dot) product, angle between vectors, projection, vector (cross) product, and applications.
content_html:
Dot (Scalar) Product

For vectors u = (u₁,u₂,u₃) and v = (v₁,v₂,v₃):

u · v = u₁v₁ + u₂v₂ + u₃v₃

Also: u · v = |u||v|cos(θ) where θ is the angle between them.

Angle Between Vectors

cos(θ) = (u · v) / (|u| |v|)

Example: u=(1,2,3), v=(4,-1,0)
u·v = 4-2+0 = 2
|u| = √14,  |v| = √17
cos(θ) = 2/(√14·√17) = 2/√238 ≈ 0.130  → θ ≈ 82.5°

Orthogonality

Vectors u and v are orthogonal (perpendicular) when u · v = 0.

Projection

The projection of u onto v:

proj_v(u) = (u·v / |v|²) v    (vector projection)
scalar component = u·v / |v|   (scalar projection)

Cross (Vector) Product

For 3D vectors u = (u₁,u₂,u₃) and v = (v₁,v₂,v₃):

u × v = |i   j   k |
        |u₁  u₂  u₃|
        |v₁  v₂  v₃|
      = (u₂v₃-u₃v₂)i − (u₁v₃-u₃v₁)j + (u₁v₂-u₂v₁)k

Also: |u × v| = |u||v|sin(θ)

Properties of Cross Product

  • u × v is perpendicular to both u and v (right-hand rule for direction).
  • u × v = −(v × u) (anti-commutative).
  • u × u = 0
  • |u × v| = area of parallelogram formed by u and v.

Gram-Schmidt Process

Converts a set of linearly independent vectors {v₁, v₂, ..., vₙ} into an orthonormal basis {e₁, e₂, ..., eₙ}:

u₁ = v₁
u₂ = v₂ − (v₂·u₁/|u₁|²)u₁         (subtract projection)
u₃ = v₃ − (v₃·u₁/|u₁|²)u₁ − (v₃·u₂/|u₂|²)u₂

Then normalize: eᵢ = uᵢ / |uᵢ|

Applications

  • Work: W = F · d = |F||d|cos(θ)
  • Torque: τ = r × F (perpendicular to both r and F)
  • Angular velocity: v = ω × r (velocity of point r rotating with angular velocity ω)
